# Speed Matrix results - full runs 2026-07-18 (29670857386) and 2026-07-19 (29675354782, current grid below; repeated tests reproduced run 1 within noise)

Run: https://github.com/interactivetools-com/SmartString/actions/runs/29675354782
(25 cells: PHP 8.1-8.5 x linux-x64/linux-arm/windows-x64/macos-x64/macos-arm,
scale=1.0, JIT off). Generated by speed-probe.php per cell, merged by
speed-merge.php; see speed-probe.php for what each test measures.

To regenerate: dispatch the Speed Matrix workflow (Actions tab or
`gh workflow run speed-matrix.yml`), download the probe-* artifacts, then
`php speed-merge.php probes/*.json`.

Verdicts from this run (details in the per-test rows below):

### Adopted

- **gate-preg** (identity fast path): SmartString 3.0 - see ENCODE_SKIP_REGEX in
  src/SmartString.php. Positive on all 25 cells.
- **arr-get** (folded __get): SmartArray 3.0 - see __get() in
  src/SmartArrayBase.php; union return kept (arr-get-mixed gain does not
  reproduce cross-platform).
- **prop-type**: SmartString 3.0 - $rawData is untyped (typed ctor param +
  ImmutabilityTest cover the contract); see its docblock.

### Rejected

- **gate-adaptive** (strspn on 8.4+): loses on short strings on every platform,
  including 8.4+.
- **promo**: tie everywhere - constructor promotion is pure style, settled.
- **memo-mix**: tie-to-slower everywhere - memo cache is pure overhead.

### Deferred (phase-2 candidates, revisit with numbers in hand)

- **tier-strrep**: str_replace tier for dirty ASCII HTML - 3-5x Windows,
  2-2.3x Linux x64, 1.4-1.6x Linux ARM, ~1.0x macOS with two consistent
  exceptions (both runs): macOS ARM 8.5 is 2.0x (its htmlspecialchars is slower),
  macOS Intel 8.1 is 0.82-0.87x (only negative cell). tier-os-gated-* proves a
  PHP_OS_FAMILY gate costs nothing where live, but gating the tier off Darwin
  would forfeit the macOS ARM 8.5 win - if adopted, prefer ungated everywhere.
- **gate-unicode**: /u clean-multibyte tier - 3.4-14.5x on accented 1KB text,
  costs ~60ns on short accented strings; matters for non-English installs.
- **gate-hybrid-len** (measured 2026-07-19): route long strings to strspn on
  PHP 8.4+ (`PHP_VERSION_ID >= 80400 && strlen >= 256`). On 8.1-8.3 every cell
  is an exact tie - the version branch provably compiles away. On 8.4+: 1.3-2.2x
  on 1KB clean, +4-9% on the realistic mix, 0.91-0.98x on shorts (the strlen
  guard's cost). scan-cross-* locates the crossover: strspn already wins at 64B
  on darwin-x64/linux-arm/windows, at ~128B on darwin-arm/linux-x64; threshold
  256 is safe on every platform.
- **stack-mix / stack-accented-mix** (measured 2026-07-19): the ceiling if all
  tiers adopt - realistic mix 2.4-2.6x on macOS/Linux ARM, 3.8x Linux x64,
  7-8x Windows (vs 1.9-2.5x for the shipped gate alone); accented mix 3.3-14.4x.
- **idiom**: ->htmlEncode() is a few percent faster than (string) cast -
  pending docs tip, not a code change.

### Reference (informs decisions, not a candidate)

- **strspn-cliff**: documents why gate-adaptive exists at all - strspn is ~50x
  slower than preg scanning before PHP 8.4 on every platform, then flips to
  1.5-2.2x faster at 8.4.
- **no-object**: ceiling measurement (~2x) for a hypothetical no-object opt-in
  class; parked, no design.

## Speed matrix: B-vs-A ratios (>1.00 = candidate faster)

<details><summary>Test legend (A vs B)</summary>

- **promo**: manual assignment vs ctor promotion
- **prop-type**: typed readonly prop vs untyped prop (typed ctor)
- **gate-preg-short**: htmlspecialchars vs preg identity gate
- **gate-preg-1kb**: htmlspecialchars vs preg identity gate
- **gate-preg-mix**: htmlspecialchars vs preg identity gate
- **gate-miss-short**: htmlspecialchars vs gate (always misses)
- **gate-miss-1kb**: htmlspecialchars vs gate (always misses)
- **strspn-cliff**: preg gate scan vs strspn scan
- **gate-adaptive-short**: preg-only gate vs version-adaptive gate
- **gate-adaptive-1kb**: preg-only gate vs version-adaptive gate
- **tier-strrep**: htmlspecialchars vs gated str_replace tier
- **gate-unicode**: two-tier (bails on UTF-8) vs three-tier (/u pass)
- **gate-hybrid-short**: shipped preg gate vs hybrid-len gate
- **gate-hybrid-1kb**: shipped preg gate vs hybrid-len gate
- **gate-hybrid-mix**: shipped preg gate vs hybrid-len gate
- **scan-cross-10**: preg scan 10B vs strspn scan 10B
- **scan-cross-64**: preg scan 64B vs strspn scan 64B
- **scan-cross-128**: preg scan 128B vs strspn scan 128B
- **scan-cross-256**: preg scan 256B vs strspn scan 256B
- **scan-cross-512**: preg scan 512B vs strspn scan 512B
- **tier-os-gated-short**: ungated two-tier vs OS-gated two-tier
- **tier-os-gated-dirty**: ungated two-tier vs OS-gated two-tier
- **stack-mix**: htmlspecialchars vs three-tier stacked
- **stack-accented-mix**: htmlspecialchars vs three-tier stacked
- **arr-get**: current 3-call chain vs folded single-lookup __get
- **arr-get-mixed**: tuned, union return vs tuned, mixed return
- **no-object**: object + __toString vs direct encoded string
- **idiom**: (string) cast vs ->htmlEncode()
- **memo-mix**: gate only vs gate + memo cache

</details>
